data.narrative:
Type One Energy is a Series B nuclear energy company that has raised over $100M and carries a current post-money valuation of $200M+. The nuclear energy sector comprises a competitive peer universe of 11 comparable companies: 3 public (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

Stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable valuation approach, applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage advancement to the $200M last post-money valuation, yielding $380M. A peer-median approach was not viable due to insufficient narrow comparables; while 7 same-sector peers exist, none satisfied the filters for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier clustering needed for statistical reliability. Reliance on an all-sector median would introduce unwarranted noise given nuclear energy's specialized dynamics.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $323M to $437M, with $380M as the median point. This range reflects near-term valuation progression within the Series B-to-later-stage window but carries material uncertainty around nuclear technology commercialization timelines, regulatory pathways, and the absence of reliable private peer pricing data in this narrow subsector. Investors should weight the stage-progression result cautiously pending tighter peer comparable data or material operational milestones that would substantiate valuation inflection.
